vocab.prompt: Copilot works best with precise function signatures and JSDoc comments as context. Write the function name, parameters, and a comment describing the behavior — Copilot completes the body. Use Copilot Chat for multi-step tasks.
vocab.context: Copilot reads all open files as context. Keep relevant files open in tabs. Use .github/copilot-instructions.md to give persistent project instructions. Copilot respects your existing code style from open files.
vocab.workflow: For new features: write a comment block describing what you want, then let Copilot autocomplete. Press Tab to accept, Esc to reject. Cycle through suggestions with Alt+]. Use Copilot Chat (Ctrl+I) for larger changes.
vocab.review: Always review Copilot suggestions — it generates plausible but sometimes incorrect code. Never accept API calls to libraries you have not verified are installed. Run tests after accepting suggestions.
vocab.chat: In Copilot Chat, use /fix for bug fixes, /explain for code explanations, /tests to generate tests, /doc to generate documentation. Use @workspace to ask questions about your entire project.
